scope.summary: Implement a 3D-viewport seam authoring mode that enables spatial edge selection on draped panels in the 3D viewport and routes the selection to SeamSpec creation, supporting all sew variants (Segment, Free, 1:N, M:N) via the same SeamSpec authority surface used by the 2D authoring path
acceptance_criteria: The 3D viewport exposes a Sewing Tool mode that accepts two edge selections (via stable element ids per GLOBAL-BUILD-041) on different panels and converts the spatial selection into a SeamSpec (Segment kind by default)
A Free Sewing modifier in the 3D viewport mode accepts normalized arc-length sub-range values [0.0,1.0] on either selected edge, producing SeamEndpoint::range values
The 3D sewing interaction emits a TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded (seam-addition) event to the EventLedger, identical to the CRDT path used by 2D panel edits (MT-135)

The model-first API exposes an equivalent create_seam_3d_selection endpoint accepting (panel_id_a, edge_index_a, range_a, panel_id_b, edge_index_b, range_b, sew_kind, gather_ratio) producing a SeamSpec — usable by swarm agents operating from solver-state snapshots without a 2D pattern view
Spatial edge selection uses the raycast / closest-edge-to-ray algorithm against the simulated mesh positions, not the 2D pattern layout
All 3D sew variants route to the same SeamSpec data model, validated identically to 2D-authored seams
proof_targets: Applies to HBR-VIS: headless capture of the 3D viewport with a newly created 3D seam highlighted as an overlay (MT-245)
scope.risk_if_missed: In MD 3D sewing is not just a convenience duplicate of 2D tools — it is the primary workflow for connecting panels that are already draped on the avatar and whose 2D edge correspondences are not visually obvious. Trousers, curved bodices, and complex multi-panel constructions are routinely sewn in 3D. Without 3D sewing the authoring surface is incomplete for any garment that cannot be fully reasoned about in the flat 2D pattern view, which breaks the production workflow for complex garments.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
